data.title: Tan Chong International Sets 2026 AGM to Approve Dividend, Director Re-Elections and Share Issuance Mandate
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Tan Chong International Limited ( (HK:0693) ) has shared an update.

Tan Chong International Limited has convened its twenty-ninth annual general meeting for 22 May 2026 in Hong Kong, where shareholders will be asked to receive and adopt the audited consolidated financial statements for the year ended 31 December 2025 and to approve a final dividend. The meeting will also consider re-election of three executive directors, authorization for the board to set directors’ fees, re-appointment of KPMG as auditors with board-determined remuneration, and a general mandate empowering directors to issue up to 20% of the company’s share capital, reinforcing governance continuity and financial flexibility for future corporate actions.

The proposed share issuance mandate, which excludes treasury shares and certain employee and rights-related issuances, would give the board additional capacity to raise capital or pursue strategic opportunities without calling a separate shareholders’ meeting for each transaction. These resolutions, if approved, will maintain the company’s existing leadership structure, streamline decision-making on director compensation and audit matters, and position Tan Chong International to respond more quickly to market conditions while balancing shareholder oversight through standard annual renewal of these authorities.

More about Tan Chong International Limited

Tan Chong International Limited is a Bermuda-incorporated investment holding company listed in Hong Kong, with operations that typically span automotive distribution and related businesses across Asia. The group focuses on managing its portfolio through a board-led governance structure, with executive directors overseeing strategy, operations and capital management to support long-term shareholder value.

Average Trading Volume: 37,202

Current Market Cap: HK$3.06B

data.title: Ping An Healthcare Delivers Strong Q1 2026 Profit Growth on Insurance Synergy and AI-Driven Health Services
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Ping An Healthcare and Technology Company ( (HK:1833) ) just unveiled an update.

Ping An Healthcare and Technology Company reported first-quarter 2026 revenue of RMB1.16 billion, up 9.1% year on year, with net profit more than doubling to RMB79.1 million and adjusted net profit rising 45.8% to RMB84.4 million. Management attributed the performance to stronger integration with Ping An Group’s insurance business and rapid expansion in corporate health management.

The company deepened mutual empowerment with Ping An Group by embedding enhanced membership health services into insurance products to boost product appeal and scenario-based marketing. In corporate health management, paying corporate clients over the past 12 months surpassed 7,500, nearly 89% higher year on year, while GMV continued to grow.

Ping An Healthcare expanded its “online, in-hospital, in-home, and in-company” service system, with AI Doctor users exceeding 5.6 million and its QR code payment network for offline pharmacy purchases reaching 111,000 outlets nationwide. Home-based senior care users climbed above 290,000, and new workplace clinics were added to improve employees’ on-site service experience.

The Group also strengthened its AI capabilities, using a combined “AI + real doctors” approach to improve medical service quality and efficiency, with complex disease treatment plans from its MDT platform achieving about a 95% accuracy rate. These advances are intended to differentiate its offerings and support broader application of AI in professional medical services and product innovation within its “insurance + health care” model.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1833) stock is a Hold with a HK$12.40 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Ping An Healthcare and Technology Company stock, see the HK:1833 Stock Forecast page.

More about Ping An Healthcare and Technology Company

Ping An Healthcare and Technology Company Limited, listed in Hong Kong, operates in the digital healthcare and health management industry, focusing on an “insurance + health care” synergistic model. The Group provides online medical services, corporate health management, and scenario-based health solutions, targeting individual users, insurers and enterprise clients across China.

Average Trading Volume: 9,861,982

Technical Sentiment Signal: Hold

Current Market Cap: HK$26.48B

data.description:

The latest update is out from YSB Inc. ( (HK:9885) ).

YSB Inc. has called its annual general meeting for 21 May 2026 in Guangzhou, where shareholders will review the audited financial statements for the year ended 31 December 2025 and vote on a proposed final dividend of RMB0.110 per share. Investors will also decide on the re-election of two directors, the authorization of directors’ remuneration, the reappointment of Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu as auditor, and grant mandates allowing the board to repurchase up to 10% of issued shares and to issue additional shares, moves that could influence the company’s capital structure and shareholder returns.

The proposed share repurchase mandate provides the board with flexibility to manage capital and potentially support the share price, while the issuance mandate preserves the company’s ability to raise equity or conduct strategic transactions in line with listing rules. The combination of a cash dividend, continuity in board composition, and renewal of the external auditor underscores an emphasis on capital return, governance stability, and compliance, which will be closely watched by shareholders assessing YSB Inc.’s stewardship and future financial policy.

More about YSB Inc.

YSB Inc., incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong, operates as a public company with its shares traded on the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong. The company is governed by a board comprising executive and independent non-executive directors and is subject to Hong Kong listing rules and corporate governance requirements.

data.title: Genertec Universal Medical Unit to Issue RMB500 Million Onshore Notes to Refinance Debt
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Genertec Universal Medical Group Company ( (HK:2666) ) has shared an announcement.

Genertec Universal Medical Group said its wholly owned subsidiary, China Universal Leasing, plans to issue the first tranche of its onshore medium-term notes for 2026 in China’s interbank bond market. The notes, with an aggregate principal of up to RMB500 million, a two-year tenor and a fixed interest rate to be set via bookbuilding, will be offered to qualified domestic institutional investors.

The issuer holds an approved programme of up to RMB3 billion, of which RMB2.5 billion remains available, and intends to use the net proceeds from this tranche to repay maturing debt. The notes are expected to start trading on the PRC interbank bond market from April 27, 2026, with China CITIC Bank and Postal Savings Bank of China acting as joint lead underwriters, highlighting the group’s continued access to China’s onshore funding channels and efforts to manage its liability profile.

More about Genertec Universal Medical Group Company

Genertec Universal Medical Group Company Limited is a Hong Kong-incorporated healthcare group with operations in mainland China. Through its wholly owned subsidiary China Universal Leasing Co., Ltd., the group engages in financial leasing and related financing services to institutional clients, supporting the healthcare and related sectors in the Chinese market.

Average Trading Volume: 4,307,021

HKT Trust and HKT Limited announced that the audited consolidated financial statements for HKT Group Holdings and Hong Kong Telecommunications for the year ended 31 December 2025 have been published on the Singapore Exchange and Taipei Exchange websites. The disclosure underscores the importance of these subsidiaries to the group’s performance, with HKT Group Holdings generating nearly all of HKT Limited’s 2025 revenue and Hong Kong Telecommunications contributing about half, highlighting their central role in the company’s operational and regional growth strategy.

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Shenzhen Expressway Co ( (HK:0548) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Shenzhen Expressway said toll collection will cease on the Shuiguan Expressway section from Shuijingcun to Guanjingtoucun in Shenzhen at 24:00 on 25 April 2026, following directives from provincial and municipal authorities. The road is operated by Shenzhen Qinglong Expressway Co., in which Shenzhen Expressway holds a 50% stake, and Qinglong will hand over the related assets in line with transport authority rules.

The company stated that the end of toll operations on this section is not expected to have a material impact on its overall financial position or operating results. Investors are directed to past financial reports and monthly toll revenue disclosures for historical data on Shuiguan Expressway’s performance, suggesting that the asset’s contribution is relatively limited within the group’s broader expressway portfolio.

Shenzhen Expressway Corporation Limited is a Chinese toll-road operator based in Shenzhen, focusing on the investment, construction, and operation of expressways and related infrastructure in the People’s Republic of China. Through subsidiaries and joint ventures, the company participates in regional transportation projects that support urban connectivity and long-term traffic development in the Greater Bay Area.

data.title: Top Form Renews Key Connected Supply Deal With Major Shareholder VDV
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Top Form International Limited ( (HK:0333) ) just unveiled an update.

Top Form International Limited has entered into a seventh renewal agreement with its substantial shareholder VDV to continue supplying ladies’ intimate apparel for a further three years from 1 July 2026 to 30 June 2029. The arrangement extends a long-standing commercial relationship under a master agreement governing recurring sales to VDV.

Because VDV holds about 25.66% of Top Form and is controlled by non-executive director Herman Van de Velde, the renewed supply arrangement is classified as a continuing connected transaction under Hong Kong listing rules. As certain percentage ratios exceed 5%, the deal requires reporting, public announcement, independent shareholders’ approval and annual review, and an Independent Board Committee and financial adviser have been appointed, with a circular to be sent to shareholders by 15 May 2026.

The transactions are considered revenue in the ordinary and usual course of business and are exempt from separate transaction classification under another chapter of the Listing Rules. The renewal reinforces the stability of Top Form’s revenue base from a key shareholder-customer while subjecting the relationship to enhanced governance and minority shareholder safeguards through independent review and approval mechanisms.

More about Top Form International Limited

Top Form International Limited is a Bermuda-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong, operating in the apparel manufacturing industry with a focus on ladies’ intimate apparel. The group supplies these products to brand and retail partners, including VDV, a substantial shareholder and long-term customer, reflecting its role in the global lingerie supply chain.

Dynam Japan Holdings has set the record date for shareholder eligibility to attend and vote at its 2026 annual general meeting, which is scheduled for Thursday, 25 June 2026. Shareholders whose names appear on the company’s share register at the close of business on Friday, 22 May 2026, will be entitled to participate in the meeting.

The company also outlined procedures for investors who have yet to register their shareholdings, requiring properly completed and stamped transfer forms with accompanying share certificates to reach the Hong Kong share registrar by 4:30 p.m. on 22 May 2026. The announcement clarifies the timetable and administrative steps for voting rights, helping investors secure governance participation well ahead of the 2026 meeting.

Shui On Land ( (HK:0272) ) just unveiled an update.

Shui On Land Limited has convened its Annual General Meeting for 27 May 2026 in Hong Kong, where shareholders will be asked to receive and adopt the audited financial statements and reports for the year ended 31 December 2025. The meeting will also consider the re-election of four directors, the authorization of directors’ remuneration, the re-appointment of the external auditor, and a broad mandate empowering the board to issue new shares and dispose of treasury shares within specified limits.

The proposed general mandate to allot, issue, and deal with additional shares, as well as to sell or transfer treasury shares, would give the board greater flexibility in future capital management and potential fundraising. This authorization, alongside the routine renewal of board and auditor mandates, underscores standard corporate governance practices and could influence Shui On Land’s capacity to respond quickly to market opportunities and financing needs, affecting both capital structure and shareholder dilution over the mandate period.

More about Shui On Land

Shui On Land Limited is a Hong Kong–listed property developer incorporated in the Cayman Islands. The company operates in the real estate sector, focusing on the development, ownership, and management of property projects in mainland China and Hong Kong, and is overseen by a board that includes both executive and independent non-executive directors.
